former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =Autumn festival (October 26) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ojiharina Ma
wakahime Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine''' (おじはりなまわかひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a successor shrine of a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * Ojiharinamawakahime-no-Mikoto === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' lists "Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Mino District (Bizen)|Q11071226|WD=Q11071226|1=Mino district}} of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}, ranking it as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bizen no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' the Sōja manuscript records "Owari Harita Mawakahime Shrine," and the Yamamoto manuscript records "Junior Fourth Rank Lower Owari Harita Myōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01046 Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. Near this shrine is a mountain called "Handa-yama," said to be a mistaken form of "Harita-yama," and the name "Harita" is said to derive from this shrine. The shrine name suggests relation to the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}, also supported by nearby {{ill|lt=Obari Shrine|Q17226111|WD=Q17226111|1=Ohari Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha associated with the Owari clan. Related theories include westward migration of the Owari clan from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-period 'Engishiki Inner Shrines and Kokushi Existing Shrines' (Obari Shrine section in 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 2 Sanyō and Shikoku' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}))., and conversely eastward migration from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Were the ancient Owari clan migrants eastward from Kibi?" ('Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walks,' Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしはりなまわかひめしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おしはりなまわかひめしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =Autumn festival (October 26) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ojiharina Ma
wakahime Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine''' (おじはりなまわかひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a successor shrine of a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * Ojiharinamawakahime-no-Mikoto === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' lists "Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Mino District (Bizen)|Q11071226|WD=Q11071226|1=Mino district}} of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}, ranking it as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bizen no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' the Sōja manuscript records "Owari Harita Mawakahime Shrine," and the Yamamoto manuscript records "Junior Fourth Rank Lower Owari Harita Myōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01046 Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. Near this shrine is a mountain called "Handa-yama," said to be a mistaken form of "Harita-yama," and the name "Harita" is said to derive from this shrine. The shrine name suggests relation to the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}, also supported by nearby {{ill|lt=Obari Shrine|Q17226111|WD=Q17226111|1=Ohari Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha associated with the Owari clan. Related theories include westward migration of the Owari clan from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-period 'Engishiki Inner Shrines and Kokushi Existing Shrines' (Obari Shrine section in 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 2 Sanyō and Shikoku' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}))., and conversely eastward migration from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Were the ancient Owari clan migrants eastward from Kibi?" ('Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walks,' Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おしはりなまわかひめしんしや]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =Autumn festival (October 26) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ojiharina Ma
wakahime Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine''' (おじはりなまわかひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a successor shrine of a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * Ojiharinamawakahime-no-Mikoto === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' lists "Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Mino District (Bizen)|Q11071226|WD=Q11071226|1=Mino district}} of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}, ranking it as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bizen no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' the Sōja manuscript records "Owari Harita Mawakahime Shrine," and the Yamamoto manuscript records "Junior Fourth Rank Lower Owari Harita Myōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01046 Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. Near this shrine is a mountain called "Handa-yama," said to be a mistaken form of "Harita-yama," and the name "Harita" is said to derive from this shrine. The shrine name suggests relation to the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}, also supported by nearby {{ill|lt=Obari Shrine|Q17226111|WD=Q17226111|1=Ohari Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha associated with the Owari clan. Related theories include westward migration of the Owari clan from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-period 'Engishiki Inner Shrines and Kokushi Existing Shrines' (Obari Shrine section in 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 2 Sanyō and Shikoku' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}))., and conversely eastward migration from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Were the ancient Owari clan migrants eastward from Kibi?" ('Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walks,' Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bizen no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' the Sōja manuscript records "Owari Harita Mawakahime Shrine," and the Yamamoto manuscript records "Junior Fourth Rank Lower Owari Harita Myōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01046 Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. Near this shrine is a mountain called "Handa-yama," said to be a mistaken form of "Harita-yama," and the name "Harita" is said to derive from this shrine. The shrine name suggests relation to the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}, also supported by nearby {{ill|lt=Obari Shrine|Q17226111|WD=Q17226111|1=Ohari Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha associated with the Owari clan. Related theories include westward migration of the Owari clan from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-period 'Engishiki Inner Shrines and Kokushi Existing Shrines' (Obari Shrine section in 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 2 Sanyō and Shikoku' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}))., and conversely eastward migration from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Were the ancient Owari clan migrants eastward from Kibi?" ('Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walks,' Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

wakahime Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine''' (おじはりなまわかひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a successor shrine of a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * Ojiharinamawakahime-no-Mikoto === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' lists "Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Mino District (Bizen)|Q11071226|WD=Q11071226|1=Mino district}} of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}, ranking it as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bizen no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' the Sōja manuscript records "Owari Harita Mawakahime Shrine," and the Yamamoto manuscript records "Junior Fourth Rank Lower Owari Harita Myōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01046 Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. Near this shrine is a mountain called "Handa-yama," said to be a mistaken form of "Harita-yama," and the name "Harita" is said to derive from this shrine. The shrine name suggests relation to the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}, also supported by nearby {{ill|lt=Obari Shrine|Q17226111|WD=Q17226111|1=Ohari Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha associated with the Owari clan. Related theories include westward migration of the Owari clan from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-period 'Engishiki Inner Shrines and Kokushi Existing Shrines' (Obari Shrine section in 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 2 Sanyō and Shikoku' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}))., and conversely eastward migration from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Were the ancient Owari clan migrants eastward from Kibi?" ('Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walks,' Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Mawakahime Shrine former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県岡山市北区津島本町20-8', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja}} [[JA:尾治針名真若比咩神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17228003}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja sandou.JPG|250px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|21.00|N|133|54|22.36|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} |祭神 = Ojiharinamawakahime-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) successor shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =Autumn festival (October 26) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ojiharina Ma
wakahime Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine''' (おじはりなまわかひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a successor shrine of a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * Ojiharinamawakahime-no-Mikoto === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' lists "Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Mino District (Bizen)|Q11071226|WD=Q11071226|1=Mino district}} of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}, ranking it as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bizen no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' the Sōja manuscript records "Owari Harita Mawakahime Shrine," and the Yamamoto manuscript records "Junior Fourth Rank Lower Owari Harita Myōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01046 Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. Near this shrine is a mountain called "Handa-yama," said to be a mistaken form of "Harita-yama," and the name "Harita" is said to derive from this shrine. The shrine name suggests relation to the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}, also supported by nearby {{ill|lt=Obari Shrine|Q17226111|WD=Q17226111|1=Ohari Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha associated with the Owari clan. Related theories include westward migration of the Owari clan from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-period 'Engishiki Inner Shrines and Kokushi Existing Shrines' (Obari Shrine section in 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 2 Sanyō and Shikoku' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}))., and conversely eastward migration from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Were the ancient Owari clan migrants eastward from Kibi?" ('Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walks,' Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]]

wakahime Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine''' (おじはりなまわかひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a successor shrine of a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * Ojiharinamawakahime-no-Mikoto === History === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' lists "Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Mino District (Bizen)|Q11071226|WD=Q11071226|1=Mino district}} of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}, ranking it as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bizen no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' the Sōja manuscript records "Owari Harita Mawakahime Shrine," and the Yamamoto manuscript records "Junior Fourth Rank Lower Owari Harita Myōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=01046 Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. Near this shrine is a mountain called "Handa-yama," said to be a mistaken form of "Harita-yama," and the name "Harita" is said to derive from this shrine. The shrine name suggests relation to the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}, also supported by nearby {{ill|lt=Obari Shrine|Q17226111|WD=Q17226111|1=Ohari Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha associated with the Owari clan. Related theories include westward migration of the Owari clan from OwariRecorded in the Meiji-period 'Engishiki Inner Shrines and Kokushi Existing Shrines' (Obari Shrine section in 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 2 Sanyō and Shikoku' ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}))., and conversely eastward migration from Kibi to OwariHiroaki Shimoyama, "Were the ancient Owari clan migrants eastward from Kibi?" ('Okayama Prefecture Mystery Walks,' Shinjinbutsu Bunko).. However, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was formerly worshiped at the present Misaki-gū site (Kitagata, Kita-ku, Okayama; {{ウィキ座標|34|41|05.76|N|133|56|04.24|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|位置|name=Misaki-gū (former site)}}), where Amatsu Shrine had been worshiped[http://www.genbu.net/data/bizen/ojiharina_title.htm Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Genmatsuko personal site).. After entering Meiji, Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine was moved here and Amatsu Shrine became a precinct end shrine. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja honden.JPG|Honden File:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden === Festivals === * Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 26|Q2953|WD=Q2953|1=October 26}}) === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Tsushimahonmachi 20-8 :: It stands on a site about 300 m up the mountain behind {{ill|lt=Meisei Gakuin High School|Q11512631|WD=Q11512631|1=Meisei Gakuin HIgh School}}. === Notes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Haiden explanatory signboard * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsushima Village section === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ojiharinamawakahime-jinja|Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine}} *[https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16489/ Ojiharinamawakahime Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ojiharina Mawakahime Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kita-ku, Okayama]]
